raw_consumer_statement:
re : Barclay 's Case Number : XXXX I was recently the victim of a scam that purported to offer me employment. The scam involved a job interview, a presentation of benefits, an employment offer letter, a W4 submission, and ( importantly ) a request to purchase my equipment for this new C-level role. 

I complied with the requests and, on XX/XX/24, approved a transaction of {$7200.00} via XXXX to cover some of the equipment my new company would be providing ( a new XXXX, XXXX, XXXX XXXX, and XXXX ). I even completed a reimbursement request form ( also enclosed ) for this purchase. 

Despite a shipping invoice to prove my purchase ( also enclosed ) I NEVER received either the equipment or the reimbursement promised. 

After some further communication with the scammers, I realized that a scam was taking place and immediately ceased communication.

But this was not until after I had 1 ) resigned from my job and 2 ) submitted large transactions for the purchase of equipment.

Following the advice of the FTC, I submitted a dispute for the charge incurred on XX/XX/24. After summarizing the issue TWICE to Barclay 's phone representatives, I received a denial of the dispute letter.

I called the phone number in the letter to request a callback from a manager, who informed me there was nothing the dispute team could do as the purchase was via a payment processor. Nor ( more importantly ) could they direct me to anyone who could provide any support.

So not only was I a victim of a crime, but now Barclay 's is demanding I pay back the money stolen with accrued interest.
